articles.title: German ZEW Sentiment Sinks to -17.2 as Iran War Hits More Than Just Energy Costs
articles.expand: Germany’s economic sentiment deteriorated sharply in April, with the ZEW Economic Sentiment index plunging from -0.5 to -17.2, well below expectations of -6.7, and marking the lowest level since late 2022.The weakness is not limited to expectations. articles.title: Fed Chair nominee Warsh to reiterate importance of independence
articles.expand: Kevin Warsh, United States (US) President Donald Trump's nominee to replace Jerome Powell as the next chair of the Federal Reserve (Fed), will testify before the Senate Banking Committee on Tuesday.

articles.expand: Prior -0.5 Current conditions -73.7 vs -70.0 expected Prior -62.9 That's the worst reading since December 2022 as the fallout from the US-Iran conflict continues to weigh heavily on German investor morale. articles.title: NZD: Stagflation risks weigh on kiwi – Commerzbank
articles.expand: Commerzbank’s Volkmar Baur notes New Zealand inflation at 3.1% year-on-year, slightly above the Reserve Bank of New Zealand (RBNZ) target, and warns that higher energy prices could push it further. He sees a possible rate hike in late May, which might briefly support the New Zealand Dollar (NZD).

articles.title: Gold struggles near daily low on firmer USD as traders keenly await US-Iran peace talks
articles.expand: Gold (XAU/USD) sticks to intraday losses below the $4,800 mark through the first half of the European session on Tuesday, though it holds above a one-week low touched the previous day. Investors remain skeptical about a potential US-Iran agreement amid the standoff over the Strait of Hormuz.
